01_initial_request.target_name: Knowledge-based Multi-Objective Estimation of Distribution Algorithm (KMOEDA)
01_initial_request.input_data: Large-scale scientific workflows (Directed Acyclic Graphs) and cloud computing virtual machines
01_initial_request.user_intent: To solve the reliability-constrained multi-objective workflow scheduling problem (RCMOWSP) effectively by improving global search ability and handling reliability constraints
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Minimize Total Execution Time (TET) and Total Execution Cost (TEC) while satisfying predefined reliability constraints

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Workflow scheduling in cloud environments is an emerging topic requiring optimization of time and cost. [Gap] Existing algorithms often ignore reliability constraints or lack global search ability for large-scale workflows. [Decision] To achieve effective scheduling, chose to use Mathematical Modeling to perform problem formulation on Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) and cloud resources, expecting to obtain a defined Reliability-Constrained Multi-Objective Workflow Scheduling Problem (RCMOWSP).

parameters.workflow_model: Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) G = <T, E, W, D>
parameters.resource_model: Heterogeneous virtual machines with hourly billing
parameters.objectives: Minimize Total Execution Time (TET) and Total Execution Cost (TEC)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Formalized RCMOWSP with three aspects: resource rental scheme, task allocation scheme, and task permutation.

references.relevance: Defines the common workflow model using Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs).
Describes cloud computing paradigm and pay-as-you-go manner.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The problem space needs to be reduced to mapping relationships between tasks and virtual machines. [Gap] Random initialization may lead to poor convergence. [Decision] To achieve promising initial solutions, chose to use Heuristic Initialization to perform external archive construction on task-resource mappings, expecting to obtain k+1 initial solutions.

parameters.strategy_1: All tasks deployed to the same resource (k solutions for k instances)
parameters.strategy_2: Solution constructed based on HEFT algorithm

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Initial external archive contains k+1 solutions constructed from heuristics.

references.relevance: HEFT algorithm used for constructing one initial solution.
Demonstrates that solid initial solutions accelerate convergence.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Global search ability needs improvement to ensure population diversity. [Gap] Conventional evolutionary algorithms may lose structural information. [Decision] To achieve diverse population, chose to use Estimation of Distribution Algorithm (EDA) to perform probability model construction and trial population generation on task-resource mappings, expecting to obtain new candidate solutions.

parameters.probability_model: Univariate model P reflecting probability task ti deployed to resource rj

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Trial population generated based on probability model and reference templates from external archive.

references.relevance: Foundation of Estimation of Distribution Algorithm (EDA).
Population-based incremental learning (PBIL) technique for updating model.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Many schedules do not meet the reliability constraint. [Gap] Existing algorithms produce too many unfeasible solutions. [Decision] To achieve feasible schedules, chose to use Reliability-aware Local Search to perform constraint handling on solutions violating reliability, expecting to obtain feasible solutions near infeasible ones.

parameters.constraint_conversion: Reliability constraint converted to weighted execution time constraint
parameters.resource_sorting: Resources sorted according to Delta wt(rj)
parameters.modification: Modify instance of resources with maximum Delta wt(rj)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Infeasible solutions modified until reliability meets user-defined constraint.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Elite non-dominated solutions need further improvement. [Gap] Standard search may not deeply explore problem space around elites. [Decision] To achieve improved elite quality, chose to use Elite Enhancement Strategy to perform task and resource perturbation on external archive solutions, expecting to obtain enhanced non-dominated solutions.

parameters.operator_1: Task perturbation (move random task on critical path to random resource)
parameters.operator_2: Resource perturbation (move all tasks on random resource to another VM)
parameters.frequency: Performed on two random solutions in L each iteration

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Two improved elite solutions generated per iteration.

references.relevance: Reference template-based sampling approach introduced in global search context.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Algorithm performance depends on parameter settings. [Gap] Optimal parameter configuration is unknown. [Decision] To achieve optimal performance, chose to use ANOVA/Parameter Calibration to perform full factorial design on KMOEDA parameters, expecting to obtain best parameter values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Best parameters identified: ps=10, rs=40, d=0.1, alpha=0.4.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Core components contribution needs verification. [Gap] Impact of initialization, global search, local search, and elite enhancement is unquantified. [Decision] To achieve component validation, chose to use Ablation Analysis to perform comparison of KMOEDA variants (KNI, KNG, KNL, KNR) on benchmark workflows, expecting to obtain hypervolume metrics for each variant.

parameters.variants: KNI (random init), KNG (no global search), KNL (no elite enhancement), KNR (no local search)
parameters.benchmarks: CyberShake, Inspiral, Montage, Sipht with 100 tasks

02_agent_trajectory.observation: KMOEDA average hypervolume superior to all variants; Global search strategy has largest impact.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Effectiveness against existing algorithms needs verification. [Gap] Performance relative to state-of-the-art multi-objective algorithms is unknown. [Decision] To achieve comparative validation, chose to use Comparative Experiment to perform evaluation of KMOEDA against EMS-C, NSGA-II, MOHEFT, ch-PICEA-g, I_MaOPSO on scientific workflows, expecting to obtain hypervolume and set coverage metrics.

parameters.workflows: CyberShake, Inspiral, Montage, Sipht (30 to 1000 tasks)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: KMOEDA average hypervolume superior to other algorithms on most instances; Set coverage shows KMOEDA dominates others.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Performance disparity needs statistical verification. [Gap] Observed differences might be due to randomness. [Decision] To achieve statistical significance confirmation, chose to use Wilcoxon's Test to perform significance testing between KMOEDA and compared algorithms, expecting to obtain p-values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: p-values between KMOEDA and other algorithms are less than 0.05, indicating significant superiority.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Comparative Experiment with Hypervolume and Set Coverage Metrics plus Wilcoxon's Statistical Test

hypervolume.value: Superior to competitors on most instances
hypervolume.interpretation: Closer to 1.44 indicates better Pareto set quality.

set_coverage.interpretation: Closer to 1 indicates KMOEDA solutions dominate others.

statistical_significance.interpretation: KMOEDA is significantly superior with 95% confidence level.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The initial objective was achieved. KMOEDA significantly outperforms several relative multi-objective workflow scheduling approaches in solving the RCMOWSP, effectively minimizing TET and TEC while satisfying reliability constraints.
